content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 6, 1989

David H. Kirkpatrick
National Economic Development
& Law Center
1950 Addison Street
Berkeley, CA 94704

Re: Letter No. 89-144

Dear Mr. Kirkpatrick:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on March 3, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Scott Hallabrin an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec 18329.)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: An advice request was submitted to the FPPC under the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question is not detailed in this acknowledgment letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an acknowledgment/receipt letter rather than an advice letter with Q&A sections. No substantive question or conclusion is present. The actual advice request content is not included in this document. Document references Letter No. 89-144.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC acknowledgment letter dated March 6, 1989, confirming receipt of an advice request from David H. Kirkpatrick of the National Economic Development & Law Center on March 3, 1989. The letter outlines the timeline and process for responding to the request.
